'''Pandemic Studios''' was a [[Wikipedia:Video game developer|video game developing]] company based in [[Wikipedia:Los Angeles, California|Los Angeles]], [[Wikipedia:California|California]] and [[Wikipedia:Brisbane|Brisbane]], [[Wikipedia:Australia|Australia]]. It was founded in [[1998]] by [[Wikipedia:Josh Resnick|Josh Resnick]] and Andrew Goldman. Pandemic was responsible for developing several ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]]s, including [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'']], ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''.

On [[November 3]], [[2005]], Pandemic Studios announced a partnership with [[BioWare]], the developers of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. Though they had common ownership and pooled their expertise to develop games, both companies retained their individual staffs and brands.

In [[2006]], Pandemic employed over 250 programmers, artists, and designers.

On November 17, 2009, [[Electronic Arts]] announced the closure of Pandemic Studios.

==Products==
*[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' (video game)]]
*''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''
*''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''
